title: Real-case portfolio table rows are not navigable to the case detail
expected: The one view that searches/filters/sorts across all my real cases lets me open a case from the row I filtered to.
got: The '§ VI — Case portfolio' CaseList runs on real cases (getCases -> getCasesForUser) with full search/filter/sort/CSV, but its <tr> rows render no link/onClick — only the smaller YourCasesCard rows deep-link to /dashboard/cases/[id]. So a 40-matter operator filters to the aging pile, then must scroll back to a different, unfiltered list to actually open a case.
suggested_acceptance: Each CaseList row links to /dashboard/cases/[id] (or YourCasesCard gains the same filter/sort so the navigable list is also the triage list).
l2_priority: Confirm live that a filtered/sorted portfolio row cannot be opened, forcing a context switch back to YourCasesCard.

title: No aging / target-date / staleness signal on any Gloria-reachable real-case view
expected: Across 40 matters I triage by what's aging — a deadline column and a 'rotting in Drafting/Review' flag on the board I can actually open.
got: Real cases persist targetFileDate:"" and attorney:"" so the portfolio's Target-file column is blank and sort-by-target-date is a no-op. The queue-age/staleness badges that exist (v0.13.0) live only in the attorney review queue (queue-age.ts / ReviewQueueView.tsx), which gates on isConfiguredAttorney and is walled out for a non-attorney — so there is no aging/triage-by-staleness signal on any surface she can reach.
suggested_acceptance: An age/last-activity (or target-date) signal is derivable and rendered on an owner-reachable case view so aging matters surface; persisted cases carry a real target/last-activity timestamp.
l2_priority: Verify live that an owner cannot see any case-age/staleness cue on /dashboard or the case detail; confirm the only aging UI is the attorney-gated queue.

title: DraftStudio idle helper copy hardcodes 'O-1A' regardless of classification
expected: On an EB-1A or O-1B matter the studio describes drafting that classification's letter.
got: The idle/error helper text reads 'Draft a full O-1A petition letter from your scored criteria…' for every classification; the generated draft itself is correctly pack-driven, so this is copy drift only.
suggested_acceptance: The helper copy interpolates the case classification (or drops the visa code).
l2_priority: Low — visual confirm on an EB-1A case.

title: Stale 'eight O-1A criteria' doc comments while the code is fully pack-driven
expected: Comments match the multi-product, classification-driven behavior.
got: EvidenceVault header comment says 'eight O-1A criteria' and lib/data/evidence.ts says docs are 'AI-categorized into an O-1A criterion', but the runtime uses criteriaNames(classification)/packFor(classification). Comment drift only; behavior is correct.
suggested_acceptance: Comments reference the case's pack/classification, not O-1A specifically.

title: First-touch lead previews are deterministic keyword-mock, not the model, even with LLM_ENGINE=claude
expected: When pre-screening a referred lead, the headline read uses the AI, not a keyword heuristic.
got: Both anonymous previews — homepage InstantVerdict (/api/qualify/preview) and the /qualify BestPathFinder (/api/qualify/preview/best-path -> recommendBestPath -> mockQualification) — run the deterministic keyword mock by design, never the model. Honest (labeled source:'mock', no charge, free-read design), but the first read is a regex, not an AI screen; the model read is behind the authenticated /api/qualify.
suggested_acceptance: Either label the preview clearly as a keyword teaser in the UI (not an AI verdict), or offer an authenticated one-click 'run the real screening' from the preview result; no fabrication either way.
l2_priority: Judge live whether the keyword preview meaningfully under/over-scores a real pasted lead vs the authenticated model screening (grounding delta).

title: Citation discipline + live auditCitations quarantines hallucinated (Exhibit N) before it reaches an attorney
got: The draft/RFE prompts forbid inventing exhibits and only allow citing on-file numbers; auditCitations flags any cited exhibit with no matching vault doc as 'unresolved' and surfaces it in the UI — catching the exact failure mode (a bad cite reaching the attorney) that costs Gloria credibility at scale.
suggested_acceptance: Keep — do not soften the unresolved-exhibit quarantine.
l2_priority: Confirm the live model honors the (Exhibit N) discipline and the unresolved badge actually fires on a planted bad cite.

title: RFE prompt fused with the as-filed petition prose + exhibits + RFE text (G1.2 fixed)
got: buildRfePrompt now receives the as-filed petition sections (attachFiledPetition via getLatestDraft) plus criteria, vault exhibits, and the RFE notice, and asks for one section per RFE issue — structurally point-by-point against THIS petition, the opposite of a generic extraordinary-ability form letter.
l2_priority: Confirm the live RFE response quotes/tracks the actual petition language and crosswalks the deficient criterion, not just the criteria structure.

title: Monotonic never-reused exhibit ordinals + whole-vault sibling context (G2.1) + honest coverage framing (dc-evidence-02)
got: Exhibit numbers come from a high-water mark across doc_seq + surviving rows so deletes never renumber; categorize now gets a read-only existing-buckets summary for sibling consistency; the UI honestly frames coverage as 'documents present, not criterion proven' and refile as a manual move that doesn't re-check fit.
l2_priority: Confirm live that a second related doc lands in the same bucket as its sibling and exhibit numbers stay monotonic across add/delete.
